CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Continued To Dec-09-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Oct-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PENNBROOK INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Strike is denied. Plaintiffs are permitted to plead alternative theories of recovery, and therefore, are entitled to plead recovery on the grounds that they are direct beneficiaries of the contract and in the alternative, that they are third party beneficiaries of the contract. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593 Plaintiffs' inclusion of paragraphs 25, 30, and 38 in the Complaint does not result in duplicative pleading. Finally, Plaintiffs' request for punitive damages in its prayer for relief is not stricken. The request refers to the second cause of action, which is not against Defendant Pennbrook. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PENNBROOK INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend in part and denied in part. The court sustains Defendant Pennbrook Insurance Services, Inc.'s demurrer to the first cause of action with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiffs' use of the "and/or" language in the first cause of action does not apprise Defendant of the contract that Plaintiffs are basing their breach of contract cause of action on or the bases of the alleged breach. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action for negligence is denied. Plaintiffs allege that Defendant failed to properly advise Plaintiffs on their insurance issues, failed to process Plaintiffs' requests for insurance, and failed to obtain promised insurance coverage, and as a result of Defendant's negligence, Plaintiffs suffered damage. (Complaint paragraph 35-37.) Plaintiffs are granted ten days leave to amend their complaint to assert a cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. In their negligence cause of action, Plaintiffs allege that Defendant made misrepresentations about Plaintiffs' coverage, they relied on those misrepresentations, and they suffered damage. (Complaint paragraph 35-37.) Those allegations should be alleged in a separate negligent misrepresentation cause of action. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
